title: The influence of mindfulness meditation combined with progressive muscle relaxation training on the clinical efficacy and quality of life of patients with sarcopenia receiving haemodialysis: a randomised controlled trial
headline: The influence of mindfulness meditation combined with progressive muscle relaxation training on the clinical e
summary: A randomized controlled trial of mindfulness / meditation examined brain & mechanisms. There were no significant differences in the baseline values of various parameters between the two groups.
keyFindings: There were no significant differences in the baseline values of various parameters between the two groups.
limitations: In terms of the kidney disease quality of life (KDQOL Combined intervention training can improve the motor ability and quality of life of patients with sarcopenia within a short period of time.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: The influence of mindfulness meditation combined with progressive muscle relaxation training on the clinical e
aiSnippet: RCT, BMC complementary medicine and therapies (2024). There were no significant differences in the baseline values of various parameters between the two groups.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) in a brief, guided format with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
